title: N+1 query in user order listing
author: contributor
pr_description: Adds order summary to user list view.
diff:
--- a/views.py
+++ b/views.py
@@ -8,8 +8,14 @@ from models import User
 
 def user_order_summary():
+    users = User.query.all()
+    result = []
+    for user in users:
+        orders = Order.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
+        result.append({'user': user.name, 'order_count': len(orders)})
+    return result
file_context:
from models import User, Order

def user_order_summary():
    users = User.query.all()
    result = []
    for user in users:
        orders = Order.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id).all()
        result.append({'user': user.name, 'order_count': len(orders)})
    return result

gold_issues.description: N+1 query: one DB query per user to fetch orders. 1000 users = 1001 queries. Use eager loading.
